signals.reasoning: Even with the event veto cleared, BTC is still just range-bound and not giving a clean momentum trigger. It stays neutral rather than becoming an active trade.
ETH remains post-flush chop with no strong directional edge. The clearer opportunities are in select alt squeeze names, not in the major here.
SOL has some residual squeeze characteristics, but the move is too muted and structure too mixed to justify a fresh position. It stays watch-only.
GOAT is still too damaged and unstable to trust. It is no longer a clean short, but it also has not rebuilt enough structure to become a serious long.
TST has already squeezed hard and is too thin to trust as a fresh chase. With the easy part of the move likely gone, the right call is to watch rather than force.
STBL still has crowded-long characteristics, but price is holding up well enough that it is not a clean short, and the long side lacks an obvious trigger. Best left alone.
MON has recovered somewhat, but the chart is still messy and does not offer a clear continuation long or breakdown short. There are cleaner names on the sheet.

0G remains one of the strongest squeeze-long setups on the board, with heavily negative funding and still-supportive price/OI behavior now that the event veto is clear. Confidence is capped because the coin is extended and volatile, but it is still a valid long candidate.

JTO is the cleanest long on the sheet: strong negative funding, solid liquidity relative to the board, and continued upside pressure without looking as overstretched as some of the smaller squeeze names. This is the best actual long candidate here.

STABLE still has strong squeeze mechanics with deeply negative funding and continued price strength. This remains a reduced-confidence long because the move is already advanced, but with the veto cleared it is upgradeable from watch-only.
